mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates rising factory output and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of months following INDPRO growth, or Rank IC is negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals deteriorating household income and consumption prospects, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts negative 20-day returns in less than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the unemployment rate and declines in industrial production identify a synchronous contraction in labor and supply conditions that depresses aggregate earnings estimates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered quarters, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) over the main effects.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, an elevated federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing present equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur more frequently than negative returns, or the mean forward return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect strengthening residential investment and downstream consumer spending on durable goods, lifting aggregate demand and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns follow fewer than 55% of monthly HOUST increases, or the Rank IC between HOUST changes and forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding wage income and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice near-term earnings.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of months following payroll growth, or the hit rate is statistically indistinguishable from 50% (binomial p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A three-month change in the consumer price index exceeding 1.0 percentage point signals accelerating inflation that erodes real corporate margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL rises more than 1.0pp over 3 months, negative 5-day S&P 500 returns occur less than 55% of the time, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls confirm a self-reinforcing expansion in consumer spending and labor income, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of rising RSAFS and PAYEMS fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction term does not improve prediction over single-variable models (F-test p > 0.10).
